Title: AtRTP5 negatively regulates plant resistance to Phytophthora pathogens by modulating the biosynthesis of endogenous jasmonic acid and salicylic acid

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Phytophthora parasitica infection; up-regulated during biotrophic phase and peaks at 24 hpi
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In Arabidopsis detached leaves and roots, induced upon P. parasitica infection; NbRTP5 homologs also induced by P. parasitica in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense hormone signaling in plant immunity against Phytophthora pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates resistance to Phytophthora pathogens by promoting jasmonic acid biosynthesis/signaling and suppressing salicylic acid biosynthesis/signaling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T-DNA mutants rtp5-2 and rtp5-3 showed enhanced resistance to Phytophthora parasitica. 2. Overexpression of AtRTP5 increased susceptibility to P. parasitica in Arabidopsis and promoted infection by P. parasitica and P. infestans in Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Loss of AtRTP5 reduced endogenous JA and activated SA accumulation/signaling, whereas overexpression increased JA and reduced SA. 4. Nuclear localization was required for AtRTP5 biological function.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA insertion mutant analysis; overexpression; VIGS of NbRTP5 homologs; transient expression; subcellular localization with GFP and DAPI; pathogen infection assays; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; HPLC-MS/MS hormone quantification
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora parasitica infection; Phytophthora infestans infection; uninfected leaves/seedlings for transcriptome and hormone measurements
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing for mutant insertion identification; RNA-seq (Illumina HiSeq4000 PE150), accession PRJNA562027
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increased susceptibility to P. parasitica and P. infestans; silencing of NbRTP5 caused more robust growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate susceptibility gene for editing or silencing to improve resistance to Phytophthora by shifting hormone balance toward SA and away from JA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Nuclear localization is essential for susceptibility-promoting function; relocalization to plasma membrane abolished infection-promoting activity
